askNext: What exactly is being removed: data influence, output behaviour, or a reproducible response pattern?
How will the team verify forgetting rather than merely hiding the symptom?
Is selective repair cheaper than rebuilding or constraining the system another way?
unlearning.body: Forgetting is easy when there was never memory. It is harder when the memory has become weights, correlations, and business promises.
unlearning.example: "They wanted unlearning to behave like deleting a file. The model, regrettably, had learned in the more inconvenient way."
unlearning.misuse: By marketing unlearning as exact surgical erasure. In reality it is often approximate, hard to verify, and bound up with trade-offs in performance, evidence, and what counts as having truly forgotten.
unlearning.practicalMeaning: In practice it usually means expensive technical work plus an even more expensive verification question: how do you prove a system no longer knows something it was once shaped by?

translations.label: legal meaning
translations.text: A hopeful answer to the question of how one retracts influence from a model that has already absorbed it.

translations.label: what it usually means in reality
translations.text: A technically messy attempt to reduce the impact of data or behaviours after the fact.

unlearning.whyExists: Because privacy requests, legal obligations, harmful content, and contaminated data do not politely arrive before the model is built. Organisations want ways to retract influence without starting from zero.

askNext: Which sources are authoritative rather than merely available?
How are permissions, duplication, and staleness being handled before retrieval begins?
What part of this data deserves structure instead of magical summarisation?
unstructured-data.body: The fantasy is that the documents contain the answer. The reality is that they contain a bureaucracy, a timeline, and three generations of contradiction.
unstructured-data.example: "The pilot was built on unstructured data, which sounded exciting until the team found six contradictory policy PDFs and a folder named final-final-use-this."
unstructured-data.misuse: By speaking as though ingesting everything automatically creates insight. In reality it creates a fresh collection of problems involving access, cleanup, ranking, duplication, provenance, and whether the documents deserved trust in the first place.
unstructured-data.practicalMeaning: In practice it usually means the real raw material for retrieval systems, copilots, and internal search, along with the operational discovery that file shares contain both the truth and several obsolete versions of it.

translations.label: enterprise meaning
translations.text: The pile of internal material everyone ignored until AI made it sound monetisable.

translations.label: what it usually means in reality
translations.text: The messy majority of organisational knowledge that lives outside tidy schemas.

unstructured-data.whyExists: Because most organisations store more knowledge in messy artefacts than in clean databases. AI interest in unstructured data is really interest in the inconvenient majority of what companies actually know.

usage-telemetry.body: Usage telemetry gets its own page because the surrounding discussion around Reasoning Tokens keeps needing this sharper distinction. Used well, it turns a vague AI-adjacent gesture into a claim someone can test, budget for, or push back on.
usage-telemetry.example: "Once usage telemetry entered the discussion, the team finally had a name for the thing everyone had been gesturing at with less precision."
usage-telemetry.misuse: It gets abused when people use the phrase as a mood, a shortcut, or a status signal instead of explaining the actual mechanism, incentive, or trade-off they mean.
usage-telemetry.practicalMeaning: In practice it usually means slowing the conversation down enough to ask whether the label points to a real system property, a commercial incentive, a user behaviour, or just a familiar rhetorical flourish.

translations.text: A useful nearby term, provided someone explains the mechanism instead of using it as scenery.
